subject: Physics
title: The Observable Universe Has Limits
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Observable Universe Has Limits usable.

hook.text: The observable universe is 93 billion light-years across, but the actual universe could be infinitely larger.

buildup.text: We can only see as far as light has traveled in 13.8 billion years. That's our cosmic horizon.

discovery.text: Beyond the horizon, there could be more galaxies, more physics—or something entirely unexpected.

twist.text: The observable universe contains about 2 trillion galaxies. What's beyond might dwarf everything we can see.

climax.text: As the universe expands, distant galaxies will eventually cross our horizon and vanish from view forever.

punchline.text: A 93-billion-light-year bubble. Our window. The rest is forever hidden.

quiz.question: Why can't we see beyond the observable universe?
options: Light from beyond hasn't had enough time to reach us since the Big Bang
There is nothing beyond the observable universe
